title: [v1.6] update containers/image
body:
Note that this includes fixes for
https://access.redhat.com/security/cve/CVE-2020-1702.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

@mheon and here's the one for 1.6.X

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/v1.6/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Changes LGTM, but lint isn't buying it.

> lint isn't buying it.

I'll take a look. Unrelated to the changes here though :(
The lint issue should be fixed now (bumped golangci-lint to 1.18). We had the same issue in master a couple of weeks ago.
Ummm. Did we ever fix the issues with bad test images for older branches? @cevich
@vrothberg Lot of non-image changes in vendor/ - is that expected/normal?

> @vrothberg Lot of non-image changes in vendor/ - is that expected/normal?

Yes, that's `go 1.13` cleaning up (I assume).

Take a peek in `.cirrus.yml`:  All the container: image: stuff should point at `quay...:$DEST_BRANCH`

This is because the "latest" tag could literally come from anywhere :grinning: 

I've already taught quay to tag images with their branch-name.

Oh, and if you want to just disable (for example) the vendor task, it can be set to ignore like this:

https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4496/files#diff-0f7067c31caedf7df2739c9b07efa122R134-R136

Eek! Probably this is important as well :disappointed_relieved: 

```diff
diff --git a/.cirrus.yml b/.cirrus.yml
index b29d4269c..bd13e8078 100644
--- a/.cirrus.yml
+++ b/.cirrus.yml
@@ -294,8 +294,8 @@ meta_task:
 # Remove old and disused images based on labels set by meta_task
 image_prune_task:
 
-    # Do not run this frequently
-    only_if: $CIRRUS_BRANCH == $DEST_BRANCH
+    # This should ONLY ever run on the master branch
+    only_if: $CIRRUS_BRANCH == "master"
 
     depends_on:
         - "meta"
```
Changed the e2e test to follow what master does to avoid timeouts.
@mheon, can you cut a new 1.6 release?

Sure.

For reference, on long term support branches branches, the release process entirely manual; I make a new branch based on the most up-to-date version of upstream 1.6, update version/version.go from 1.6.4 to 1.6.5, commit that, and then make a signed tag of that commit ID via `git tag -s -m "v1.6.5" v1.6.5 <COMMIT_HASH>`.

For master, using hack/release.sh is sufficient, unless you need to make a long-term-support branch.
